reports-alv-015.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: Interviewer trap questions wo hote hain jahan surface pe question simple lagta hai — lekin wrong answer doge toh immediately pata chal jaata hai ki tumhare concepts clear nahi hain. Reports aur ALV mein 8 classic traps hain. Pehla trap: 'ALV aur WRITE ko mix kar sakte ho ek program mein?' — answer hai NO. Dono output methods alag hain, ek saath use nahi ho sakte. Doosra trap: 'ALV Grid mein data modify ho sakta hai?' — partially correct: sirf OO ALV (CL_GUI_ALV_GRID) mein editable columns set kar sakte ho, REUSE function module mein nahi. Teesra trap: 'REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY S/4HANA mein deprecated hai?' — YES, deprecated hai, OO ALV ya SALV use karo naye development ke liye. Chautha trap: 'TOP-OF-PAGE aur TOP-OF-LIST same hai?' — bilkul nahi! TOP-OF-PAGE har page pe print hota hai, TOP-OF-LIST sirf pehle page pe. Paanchwan: 'Field catalog mandatory hai?' — technically NO, structure name se auto-build hoti hai, lekin bina explicit field catalog ke bad practice hai — column order, labels, currency reference sab manually control nahi hota. Chhata trap: 'Ek screen pe 2 ALV grids ho sakte hain?' — YES, container aur splitter use karo. Saatwan: 'SELECT sorted hai toh internal table bhi sorted rahega?' — NO guarantee after LOOP, APPEND, MOVE operations. Aathwan: 'Background job mein ALV grid display ho sakti hai?' — NO, background mode mein screen display nahi hoti, spool ya file output use karo.
reports-alv-015.hinglishMasterExplanation:
Interviewer trap questions basically examiner ka way hai yeh test karne ka ki tum ratta maarte ho ya actually samjhe ho. Reports aur ALV ke context mein ye 8 traps most commonly aate hain — har ek ka detailed explanation:

1. ALV aur WRITE mixing: WRITE statement classical report output method hai — spool-based, page-based rendering. ALV (both REUSE aur OO) screen-based grid display hai. Dono fundamentally different output streams hain. Ek program mein dono use karne ki koshish karo toh runtime error ya unexpected behavior aata hai. Interview answer: 'No, they use different output mechanisms and cannot be mixed in the same program.'

2. Editable ALV Grid: REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY mein editable columns ka concept nahi hai — display-only hai. CL_GUI_ALV_GRID mein specific columns ko edit_mask set karke editable banate hain, phir DATA_CHANGED event handle karte hain. Beginners yahan galat answer dete hain — wo sochte hain koi bhi ALV editable hoti hai.

3. REUSE deprecation in S/4HANA: SAP ne officially recommend kiya hai ki naye development mein REUSE function module mat use karo — OO ALV (CL_GUI_ALV_GRID) ya SALV (CL_SALV_TABLE) use karo. Ye deprecated hai matlab support limited hoga future mein.

4. TOP-OF-PAGE vs TOP-OF-LIST: TOP-OF-LIST event sirf ek baar trigger hota hai — output ke shuruat mein. TOP-OF-PAGE har naye page ki beginning pe trigger hota hai. Classical reports mein page headers ke liye TOP-OF-PAGE, report title ke liye TOP-OF-LIST. Galat use kiya toh header har page pe nahi dikhega.

5. Field catalog mandatory question: Technically REUSE mein i_structure_name deke auto-build kar sakte ho — field catalog mandatory nahi hai syntax ke liye. Lekin practically mandatory hai kyunki column labels, currency/quantity reference, column sequence, visibility control — sab field catalog se hota hai. Bina explicit field catalog ke professional-grade report banana mushkil hai.

6. Two ALV grids on one screen: Haan possible hai — CL_GUI_CUSTOM_CONTAINER aur CL_GUI_SPLITTER_CONTAINER use karo. Screen ko split karo, har split mein alag container banao, phir alag ALV grid instances banao. Master-detail layouts ke liye common pattern hai.

7. SELECT sorted = internal table sorted: SELECT mein ORDER BY lagaya toh database se sorted data aata hai. Lekin phir jab tum LOOP AT, APPEND, DELETE, INSERT karte ho internal table pe — sort order preserve nahi hoti guaranteed taur pe. Final sort karo explicitly SORT statement se before ALV display.

8. Background job + ALV display: Background mein koi screen nahi hoti — dialog workprocess nahi hota. ALV grid display attempt karoge background mein toh dump aata hai. Background reports mein SUBMIT with TO SAP-SPOOL use karo, ya file output generate karo.
reports-alv-015.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'Common ALV trap questions focus on four key areas: mixing output methods, where ALV and WRITE cannot be combined; editability, where only OO ALV with CL_GUI_ALV_GRID supports editable columns through the DATA_CHANGED event; the REUSE deprecation in S/4HANA, where new development should use OO ALV or SALV; and background job limitations, where ALV grid display is not possible since there is no screen context — spool or file output must be used instead. I also know that TOP-OF-PAGE fires on every page while TOP-OF-LIST fires only once, and that a SELECT ORDER BY does not guarantee internal table sort order after subsequent ABAP operations.'

Hinglish tip: Ye sab trap questions mein confident tone mein answer karo — hesitation mat dikhao. 'No, they cannot be mixed' ya 'Yes, but only in OO ALV' jaise clear statements interviewer ko impress karte hain. Vague answers jaise 'maybe', 'I think' — ye weak impression dete hain.
reports-alv-015.codeExamples:
" Trap 1 demonstration: Editable ALV only in OO ALV
DATA: lo_grid TYPE REF TO cl_gui_alv_grid,
      lo_cont TYPE REF TO cl_gui_custom_container.
CREATE OBJECT lo_cont EXPORTING container_name = 'CC_MAIN'.
CREATE OBJECT lo_grid EXPORTING i_parent = lo_cont.

" Make specific column editable in field catalog
ls_fcat-fieldname = 'MENGE'.   " Quantity field
ls_fcat-edit      = abap_true. " Only OO ALV supports this
APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat.

lo_grid->set_table_for_first_display(
  EXPORTING is_layout = ls_layout
  CHANGING  it_outtab      = lt_data[]
            it_fieldcatalog = lt_fcat ).

" Trap 8: Background job check — no ALV display
IF sy-batch = abap_true.  " Running in background?
  " Write to file instead of ALV display
  OPEN DATASET lv_file FOR OUTPUT IN TEXT MODE.
  LOOP AT lt_data INTO ls_data.
    TRANSFER ls_data TO lv_file.
  ENDLOOP.
  CLOSE DATASET lv_file.
ENDIF.

I also know that TOP-OF-PAGE fires on every page while TOP-OF-LIST fires only once, and that a SELECT ORDER BY does not guarantee internal table sort order after subsequent ABAP operations.

reports-alv-015.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Eight common interviewer trap questions about Reports and ALV behaviour and limits.

Used for:
- Separating rote candidates from those who truly know SAP standards in Reports and ALV
- Catching wrong assumptions about mixing WRITE with ALV and about editability in REUSE versus OO ALV
- Testing S/4HANA awareness around REUSE deprecation and recommended use of CL_SALV_TABLE or CL_GUI_ALV_GRID
- Verifying knowledge of background mode limitations and sort-order pitfalls after LOOP and MODIFY

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- CL_GUI_ALV_GRID with field catalog edit flag for editable columns, DATA_CHANGED for validation
- TOP-OF-PAGE fires on every page; TOP-OF-LIST fires once at the start of the list
- sy-batch check before any ALV display in background; use spool or OPEN DATASET file
- CL_GUI_SPLITTER_CONTAINER hosts two ALV grids on one screen for master-detail layouts

Interview keyword: confident clear answers — no maybe, no I think

followupAnswerBank.question: Can you use WRITE statement and ALV display in the same ABAP report?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Nahi, WRITE aur ALV ek hi program mein mix nahi kiye ja sakte. WRITE classical list processing use karta hai spool-based rendering ke saath, jabki ALV screen-based grid display use karta hai. Yeh fundamentally different output mechanisms pe operate karte hain. Agar tum dono attempt karte ho, tumhe unpredictable behavior ya ek runtime error milega. Ek program ko ek output method choose karna padta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: No, WRITE and ALV cannot be mixed in the same program. WRITE uses classical list processing with spool-based rendering, while ALV uses screen-based grid display. They operate on fundamentally different output mechanisms. If you attempt both, you will get unpredictable behavior or a runtime error. A program must choose one output method.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Project mein ek junior developer ne WRITE se header print kiya phir REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY call kiya — output garbled tha. Fix: header information ko ALV ki TOP_OF_LIST event mein move kiya. Always consistent output method use karo ek program mein.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Sochna ki WRITE se kuch lines print kar sakte ho aur phir ALV grid show kar sakte ho — ye technically incorrect hai aur unexpected results deta hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" WRONG approach — do not mix
" WRITE: 'Report Title'.  " This conflicts with ALV
" CALL FUNCTION 'REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY'...

" CORRECT: Use ALV TOP_OF_LIST for headers
DATA lt_listheader TYPE slis_t_listheader.
ls_header-typ  = 'H'.  " H=Header, S=Selection, A=Action
ls_header-info = 'Sales Report - Monthly Summary'.
APPEND ls_header TO lt_listheader.
CALL FUNCTION 'REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY'
  EXPORTING it_list_commentary = lt_listheader.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the difference between TOP-OF-PAGE and TOP-OF-LIST in ABAP reports?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: TOP-OF-LIST output ke beginning mein exactly ek baar fire hota hai — report title, run date aur selection criteria summary ke liye useful. TOP-OF-PAGE har page ke beginning mein fire hota hai — column headers, page numbers aur repeating header information ke liye use hota hai. Agar tum column headers ke liye TOP-OF-LIST use karte ho, woh sirf first page pe appear honge, jo subsequent pages pe users ko confuse karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: TOP-OF-LIST fires exactly once at the beginning of the output — useful for report title, run date, and selection criteria summary. TOP-OF-PAGE fires at the beginning of every page — used for column headers, page numbers, and repeating header information. If you use TOP-OF-LIST for column headers, they will only appear on the first page, which confuses users on subsequent pages.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Bug report: 'Column headers sirf pehle page pe hain, baaki pages mein nahi.' Root cause: column headers TOP-OF-LIST mein likhe the. Fix: move to TOP-OF-PAGE event. Simple fix, lekin production mein pata laga — embarrassing situation.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: TOP-OF-LIST mein column headers likhna — ye sirf pehli page pe print hote hain. Har page pe repeat karne wale elements TOP-OF-PAGE mein hone chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
REPORT zdemo_page_events.

TOP-OF-LIST.
  WRITE: / 'SALES REPORT - MONTHLY SUMMARY'.
  WRITE: / 'Generated:', sy-datum.
  " This appears ONCE at start

TOP-OF-PAGE.
  WRITE: / '| Document No | Date       | Amount |'.
  WRITE: / '|-------------|------------|--------|'.
  " This appears on EVERY page

END-OF-PAGE.
  WRITE: / 'Page:', sy-pagno.
  " Footer on every page

followupAnswerBank.question: Is REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY deprecated in S/4HANA? What should you use instead?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Haan, S/4HANA mein REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY aur related function modules deprecated hain. SAP object-oriented ALV use karne ki recommendation karta hai — ya toh CL_SALV_TABLE simpler display-only reports ke liye minimal code ke saath, ya CL_GUI_ALV_GRID full control ke liye including editable columns, custom toolbar buttons aur event handling. REUSE use karne wali existing reports work karti rehti hain, lekin new development ko OO ALV use karna chahiye SAP ki recommended architecture ke saath align karne ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Yes, REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY and related function modules are deprecated in S/4HANA. SAP recommends using object-oriented ALV — either CL_SALV_TABLE for simpler display-only reports with minimal code, or CL_GUI_ALV_GRID for full control including editable columns, custom toolbar buttons, and event handling. Existing reports using REUSE continue to work, but new development should use OO ALV to align with SAP's recommended architecture.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Code review mein ek senior reviewer ne commented: 'Why are you using REUSE_ALV in a new S/4HANA development?' Correct answer should have been CL_SALV_TABLE for this display-only report. Lesson learned: always check SAP's current recommendation for each API in new projects.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Using REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY in brand new S/4HANA development — it works but shows you are using outdated approach. Interviewers specifically ask this to differentiate candidates who know current SAP standards.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Modern approach: CL_SALV_TABLE (S/4HANA preferred)
DATA: lo_salv TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table,
      lx_msg  TYPE REF TO cx_salv_msg.
TRY.
    cl_salv_table=>factory(
      IMPORTING r_salv_table = lo_salv
      CHANGING  t_table      = lt_data ).
    lo_salv->get_columns( )->set_optimize( abap_true ).
    lo_salv->get_display_settings( )->set_striped_pattern(
      cl_salv_display_settings=>true ).
    lo_salv->display( ).
  CATCH cx_salv_msg INTO lx_msg.
    MESSAGE lx_msg->get_text( ) TYPE 'E'.
ENDTRY.

followupAnswerBank.question: Is field catalog mandatory for ALV? What happens if you skip it?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Field catalog technically optional hai jab tum i_structure_name provide karte ho — ALV framework usse DDIC structure se auto-generate karta hai. However, best practice hai hamesha ek explicit field catalog build karna. Iske bina, tum column labels, currency aur quantity reference fields, column sequence, visibility aur output key settings pe control lose kar dete ho. Auto-generated field catalogs often technical field names dikhate hain bajaaye meaningful labels ke, jisse reports unprofessional lagti hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Field catalog is technically optional when you provide i_structure_name — the ALV framework auto-generates it from the DDIC structure. However, best practice is to always build an explicit field catalog. Without it, you lose control over column labels, currency and quantity reference fields, column sequence, visibility, and output key settings. Auto-generated field catalogs often show technical field names instead of meaningful labels, making reports look unprofessional.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: UAT feedback: 'Column headers are confusing — they show technical names.' Root cause: developer used i_structure_name only, no explicit field catalog. Fix during UAT: build field catalog with proper coltext values for each field. Added 4 hours of rework that could have been avoided.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Relying on i_structure_name auto-generation for production reports — always define explicit field catalog with proper column labels, currency references, and required field settings.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Explicit field catalog — professional approach
DATA: ls_fcat TYPE slis_fieldcat_alv,
      lt_fcat TYPE slis_t_fieldcat_alv.

ls_fcat-fieldname  = 'VBELN'.  ls_fcat-coltext = 'Sales Order'.
ls_fcat-outputlen  = 12.       ls_fcat-key     = abap_true.
APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat.    CLEAR ls_fcat.

ls_fcat-fieldname  = 'NETWR'.  ls_fcat-coltext = 'Net Value'.
ls_fcat-do_sum     = abap_true.  " Enable totals
ls_fcat-cfieldname = 'WAERK'.    " Currency reference
APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat.    CLEAR ls_fcat.

CALL FUNCTION 'REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY'
  EXPORTING it_fieldcat = lt_fcat
  TABLES    t_outtab    = lt_data.

followupAnswerBank.question: Can you display two ALV grids on the same screen? How?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Haan, tum CL_GUI_SPLITTER_CONTAINER use karke ek screen pe do ALV grids display kar sakte ho. Screen pe ek splitter container create karo, rows aur columns specify karo split layout define karne ke liye, phir har area ke liye ek cell container reference paao. Har cell container ko bound ek separate CL_GUI_ALV_GRID instance create karo. Yeh commonly master-detail scenarios ke liye use hota hai — example, top grid mein sales orders ka list aur bottom grid mein selected order ke line items.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Yes, you can display two ALV grids on one screen using CL_GUI_SPLITTER_CONTAINER. Create a splitter container on the screen, specify rows and columns to define the split layout, then get a cell container reference for each area. Create a separate CL_GUI_ALV_GRID instance bound to each cell container. This is commonly used for master-detail scenarios — for example, a list of sales orders in the top grid and the line items of the selected order in the bottom grid.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Requirement: 'Purchase order list upar, selected PO ke items neeche usi screen pe.' Solution: splitter container — top half ke liye PO header ALV, bottom half ke liye PO item ALV. Double-click event on header grid triggers item grid refresh. Classic master-detail pattern.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Trying to instantiate two CL_GUI_ALV_GRID objects on the same default screen without containers — they will overlap. Always use splitter or separate custom containers for each grid.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
DATA: lo_splitter TYPE REF TO cl_gui_splitter_container,
      lo_top_cont TYPE REF TO cl_gui_container,
      lo_bot_cont TYPE REF TO cl_gui_container,
      lo_grid_hdr TYPE REF TO cl_gui_alv_grid,
      lo_grid_itm TYPE REF TO cl_gui_alv_grid.

CREATE OBJECT lo_splitter
  EXPORTING parent  = cl_gui_container=>screen0
            rows    = 2  columns = 1.

lo_top_cont = lo_splitter->get_container( row = 1 column = 1 ).
lo_bot_cont = lo_splitter->get_container( row = 2 column = 1 ).

CREATE OBJECT lo_grid_hdr EXPORTING i_parent = lo_top_cont.
CREATE OBJECT lo_grid_itm EXPORTING i_parent = lo_bot_cont.

followupAnswerBank.question: Does SELECT with ORDER BY guarantee that the internal table stays sorted after ABAP operations?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Nahi, ORDER BY ke saath SELECT SELECT ke point pe sorted data guarantee karta hai, lekin internal table pe subsequent ABAP operations — APPEND, INSERT, DELETE, COLLECT ya LOOP mein conditional processing — sort order disrupt kar sakte hain. Best practice hai hamesha internal table pe explicit SORT statement perform karna usse ALV display ko pass karne se immediately pehle, especially kisi bhi manipulation ke baad. Kabhi assume mat karo ki database sort order ABAP processing ke through preserve hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: No, SELECT with ORDER BY guarantees sorted data at the point of the SELECT, but subsequent ABAP operations on the internal table — APPEND, INSERT, DELETE, COLLECT, or conditional processing in LOOP — can disrupt the sort order. Best practice is to always perform an explicit SORT statement on the internal table immediately before passing it to ALV display, especially after any manipulation. Never assume the database sort order is preserved through ABAP processing.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Bug: 'Report mein data sorted nahi dikh raha even though SELECT mein ORDER BY hai.' Investigation: LOOP AT ke andar DELETE kuch rows kar rahe the — sort order disturb hua. Fix: SORT lt_data BY vkorg erdat at the end before ALV display. 10-minute fix for a 2-hour debugging session.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Assuming ORDER BY in SELECT is sufficient and not adding SORT before ALV display. Any intermediate ABAP table operations can break the sort order.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
SELECT vbeln, erdat, kunnr, netwr
  FROM vbak
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_sales)
  WHERE erdat >= @( sy-datum - 90 )
  ORDER BY erdat DESCENDING.  " Sorted at DB level

" Subsequent operations may disrupt order
DELETE lt_sales WHERE netwr <= 0.   " Removes rows
LOOP AT lt_sales ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls>).
  <ls>-kunnr = CONV #( <ls>-kunnr ).  " Modifies data
ENDLOOP.

" ALWAYS sort explicitly before ALV display
SORT lt_sales BY erdat DESCENDING.  " Guaranteed final order
" Now pass to ALV

followupAnswerBank.question: Can an ALV grid report run in background mode?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Nahi, ALV grid display background mode mein work nahi karta kyunki background jobs batch workprocesses mein bina kisi screen ya dialog context ke run hote hain. Background mein ALV display attempt karna runtime error cause karega. Background-compatible output ke liye, SUBMIT with TO SAP-SPOOL use karo classical list output ke liye jo SP01 mein view ho sake, ya OPEN DATASET use karke file mein data write karo. Hamesha program start pe sy-batch check karo aur background mein run karte waqt appropriate output method pe redirect karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: No, ALV grid display does not work in background mode because background jobs run in batch workprocesses without any screen or dialog context. Attempting ALV display in background will cause a runtime error. For background-compatible output, use SUBMIT with TO SAP-SPOOL for classical list output that can be viewed in SP01, or write data to a file using OPEN DATASET. Always check sy-batch at program start and redirect to appropriate output method when running in background.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Production incident: Report scheduled as background job was dumping every night. Root cause: CL_SALV_TABLE=>display( ) called without sy-batch check. Fix: IF sy-batch = abap_true, write to file; ELSE, show ALV. Simple guard clause solved the nightly dump.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Not checking sy-batch before ALV display — causes runtime dump in background jobs. Always implement sy-batch check and provide alternative output for background mode.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Always check sy-batch before ALV display
IF sy-batch = abap_true.
  " Background mode: write to file
  DATA: lv_path TYPE string VALUE '/tmp/sales_report.csv'.
  OPEN DATASET lv_path FOR OUTPUT IN TEXT MODE
    ENCODING DEFAULT.
  LOOP AT lt_data INTO DATA(ls_row).
    DATA(lv_line) = |{ ls_row-vbeln };{ ls_row-netwr }|.
    TRANSFER lv_line TO lv_path.
  ENDLOOP.
  CLOSE DATASET lv_path.
  MESSAGE 'Report written to file' TYPE 'S'.
ELSE.
  " Foreground: normal ALV display
  cl_salv_table=>factory(
    IMPORTING r_salv_table = DATA(lo_salv)
    CHANGING  t_table      = lt_data ).
  lo_salv->display( ).
ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: When an interviewer asks 'Is field catalog optional in ALV?' — what is the trap and correct answer?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Technically correct answer yeh hai ki field catalog optional hai jab i_structure_name provided hai — ALV usse DDIC definition se auto-generate karta hai. However, complete answer yeh hai ki professional development mein, ek explicit field catalog hamesha required hai. Iske bina, tum column labels, column sequence, currency aur quantity reference fields, totals enablement ya output key marking control nahi kar sakte. Auto-generation quick prototypes ke liye fine hai lekin production reports ke liye never.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The technically correct answer is that field catalog is optional when i_structure_name is provided — ALV auto-generates it from the DDIC definition. However, the complete answer is that in professional development, an explicit field catalog is always required. Without it, you cannot control column labels, column sequence, currency and quantity reference fields, totals enablement, or output key marking. Auto-generation is fine for quick prototypes but never for production reports.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Code review comment received: 'You used i_structure_name without field catalog — how will the currency reference be set for amount columns?' The reviewer was right. Without cfieldname in field catalog, amount columns show without currency symbol and totals may be incorrect across different currencies.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Answering 'field catalog is optional' without adding the best practice caveat — this gives the impression you would skip it in production code, which is a red flag for interviewers.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" AUTO-GENERATED (not recommended for production)
CALL FUNCTION 'REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY'
  EXPORTING
    i_structure_name = 'VBAK'  " Auto-builds field catalog
  TABLES t_outtab = lt_data.
" Problem: NETWR shows as 'NETWR' not 'Net Value'
" WAERK currency reference not linked to NETWR

" EXPLICIT FIELD CATALOG (recommended)
ls_fcat-fieldname  = 'NETWR'.
ls_fcat-coltext    = 'Net Value'.    " Professional label
ls_fcat-cfieldname = 'WAERK'.        " Currency reference
ls_fcat-do_sum     = abap_true.      " Enable totals
ls_fcat-outputlen  = 15.
APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat.

reports-alv-016.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: Technical design document (TDS) ek blueprint hai — jaise ghar banana start karne se pehle architect drawing banata hai. Report banane se pehle ek document banate hain jisme sab kuch clearly likha hota hai taaki developer, reviewer, aur client sab ek page pe rahein. TDS mein 9 key sections hote hain: (1) Program name aur type — ye classical report hai, interactive hai, ya ALV hai, transaction code kya hai. (2) Selection screen fields — har field ka technical name, data type, mandatory ya optional, default value. (3) Data selection logic — kaunsi tables se data aayega, WHERE conditions, JOINs, aur performance targets — jaise maximum kitni rows laani hain. (4) Output fields — ALV mein kaunse columns dikhenge, unke labels, width, currency references. (5) Default ALV layout — initially kaise sort hoga, kaunse subtotals honge, toolbar pe kaunse custom buttons honge. (6) User command handling — toolbar ke custom buttons pe kya hoga, double-click pe kya hoga. (7) Authorization objects — kaun kya dekh sakta hai, kaunse authority checks honge. (8) Performance targets — maximum kitni rows expected hain, acceptable runtime kya hai, koi UP TO n ROWS clause hai kya. (9) Background scheduling — kab run hogi, kaunsa variant use hogi, output kahan jaayega. Ye document hi tera save karta hai jab client baad mein bole 'hamne ye nahi maanga tha' — TDS mein signed off hai, proof hai.
reports-alv-016.hinglishMasterExplanation:
Technical design document likhna sirf documentation ka kaam nahi hai — ye project mein tumhari technical maturity show karta hai. Reports aur ALV ke liye TDS structure ka detailed breakdown karo:

Section 1 — Program Information: Program name (Z prefix, meaningful name jaise ZSALES_MONTHLY_RPT), program type (executable report, module pool), transaction code if applicable, transport number, development system.

Section 2 — Selection Screen Design: Table banao — field name, screen label, data type, length, single/range, mandatory/optional, default value, search help. Jaise: S_ERDAT (date range, optional, default last 30 days), P_BUKRS (single value, mandatory, no default).

Section 3 — Data Selection Logic: Primary table se start karo, phir JOINs document karo. Jaise: 'SELECT from VBAK JOIN VBAP ON vbeln, then JOIN KNA1 ON kunnr. WHERE ERDAT in S_ERDAT AND VKORG in S_VKORG.' Performance notes: 'Expected max 50,000 rows. UP TO 100000 ROWS clause. SE16N verification table: VBAK.'

Section 4 — Output Field Catalog: Table banao — column name, source table/field, display label, width, type (char/amount/date), currency reference, total enabled, key field. Ye table hi field catalog code ka basis banta hai.

Section 5 — Default ALV Layout: Sort criteria document karo — primary sort: VKORG ascending, secondary: ERDAT descending. Subtotals: at VKORG break. Default visible columns: sab. Hidden columns: document number (searchable but hidden by default).

Section 6 — User Commands/Events: Table banao — function code, description, triggered by, action. Jaise: ZDET (Double-click → open sales order display), ZSUM (Button 'Summary View' → switch to summary), ZEXP (Button 'Export' → custom Excel with formatting).

Section 7 — Authorization Objects: List karo — S_TABU_DIS for SE16N access during testing, F_VBRK_BUK for billing documents, M_MATE_BUK for materials. AUTHORITY-CHECK statement kahan hoga code mein.

Section 8 — Performance Targets: Max rows, expected runtime (jaise under 30 seconds for 10k rows), index recommendations, ST05 trace expected results.

Section 9 — Background Scheduling: Variant name, schedule frequency, output: spool ya file, email notification, retention period.
reports-alv-016.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'For a Reports and ALV technical design document, I document nine key sections: program metadata including name, type, and transaction code; selection screen fields with data types and mandatory flags; data selection logic covering table joins, WHERE conditions, and performance targets such as maximum row limits; output field catalog with column labels, widths, currency references, and totals configuration; default ALV layout including sort criteria and subtotals; user command handling for custom toolbar buttons and double-click actions; authorization objects and where AUTHORITY-CHECK is placed in code; performance targets with expected runtime; and background scheduling approach with variant name and output destination. This document serves as the signed-off baseline that prevents scope creep during development and testing.'

Hinglish tip: Interview mein TDS ke 9 sections ek ek bolne ki zaroorat nahi — main sections bolte hue emphasis lagao: selection screen design, field catalog definition, aur authorization objects. Ye 3 cheezon pe detail doge toh interviewer samjhega ki tum structured developer ho.
reports-alv-016.codeExamples:
" TDS-driven field catalog — each row maps to TDS output table

" Build field catalog from TDS specification
DEFINE m_fcat.

  ls_fcat-do_sum    = &5. ls_fcat-cfieldname = &6.
  APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat. CLEAR ls_fcat.
END-OF-DEFINITION.
" TDS Field          Label          Wid Key Sum CurRef
m_fcat 'VKORG'  'Sales Org'       6  'X' ''  ''.
m_fcat 'VBELN'  'Sales Order'     12 'X' ''  ''.
m_fcat 'NETWR'  'Net Value'       15 ''  'X' 'WAERK'.

This document serves as the signed-off baseline that prevents scope creep during development and testing.

reports-alv-016.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Nine-section technical design document template for Reports and ALV deliveries.

Used for:
- Locking scope with the functional consultant before any coding starts
- Mapping selection screen fields, output field catalog and authorization checks one-to-one with code
- Defending against UAT scope creep by holding a signed-off baseline
- Communicating performance targets and background scheduling decisions to BASIS and functional teams

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- Selection screen table maps directly to PARAMETERS and SELECT-OPTIONS in code
- Output field table drives slis_t_fieldcat_alv or lvc_t_fcat row build
- Authorization section becomes the literal AUTHORITY-CHECK statements
- Default layout section drives slis_t_sortinfo_alv with subtot and spos
- Background scheduling section names the variant used in SM36 and confirms sy-batch handling

Interview keyword: TDS-driven field catalog and signed-off scope

followupAnswerBank.question: What are the mandatory sections in a Reports and ALV technical design document?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Mandatory sections hain: program information including name aur type; selection screen fields technical names, types aur mandatory flags ke saath; data selection logic covering primary tables, joins aur WHERE conditions; output field catalog specifying columns, labels aur data types; aur authorization objects. Additional recommended sections mein default layout configuration, user command handling, performance targets aur background scheduling approach shamil hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The mandatory sections are: program information including name and type; selection screen fields with technical names, types, and mandatory flags; data selection logic covering primary tables, joins, and WHERE conditions; output field catalog specifying columns, labels, and data types; and authorization objects. Additional recommended sections include default layout configuration, user command handling, performance targets, and background scheduling approach.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Project mein TDS ke bina banaya tha report — UAT mein client bola 'column order galat hai, ye fields nahi chahiye the, filter pe ye field nahi hai.' Teen baar rework. Lesson: field catalog section TDS mein sabse pehle client se sign off karwao.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Skipping authorization objects section in TDS — then discovering during security testing that required auth checks were not coded. Always document auth objects upfront.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TDS Section 7: Authorization check template
" Document: AUTHORITY-CHECK for F_VBRK_BUK before data fetch
AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_VBRK_BUK'
  ID 'BUKRS' FIELD p_bukrs
  ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.   " 03 = Display
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  MESSAGE e001(z_auth) WITH p_bukrs.  " Z_AUTH message class
ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you document the selection screen design in a TDS?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Main selection screen fields ko ek table format mein document karta hoon columns ke saath field name, screen label, technical field name, data type, single value ya range, mandatory flag, default value aur assigned search help ke liye. Yeh table directly code mein PARAMETERS aur SELECT-OPTIONS statements pe map karta hai. Clients aur functional consultants is table ko ABAP knowledge ke bina review kar sakte hain aur exactly konse filters report offer karega uspe sign off kar sakte hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: I document selection screen fields in a table format with columns for field name, screen label, technical field name, data type, single value or range, mandatory flag, default value, and search help assigned. This table maps directly to PARAMETERS and SELECT-OPTIONS statements in code. Clients and functional consultants can review this table without ABAP knowledge and sign off on exactly what filters the report will offer.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Functional sign-off on TDS selection screen table ne ek issue pakda: wo chahte the plant range, not single plant. TDS review mein catch hua — code likhne se pehle. Agar code mein PARAMETERS tha SELECT-OPTIONS ke jagah, rework hota.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Documenting only field names without specifying single/range type — then coding PARAMETERS when functional wanted SELECT-OPTIONS range selection.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TDS-mapped selection screen code
" TDS Row: S_ERDAT | Order Date | ERDAT | DATS | Range | Optional
" TDS Row: P_BUKRS | Company Code | BUKRS | CHAR4 | Single | Mandatory
SELECT-OPTIONS: s_erdat FOR vbak-erdat
  DEFAULT sy-datum - 30  TO sy-datum.  " TDS default: last 30 days
PARAMETERS: p_bukrs TYPE bukrs OBLIGATORY.  " TDS: Mandatory

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you document data selection logic and performance targets in TDS?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Data selection documentation mein shamil hai: primary aur secondary tables join types aur conditions ke saath, selection screen parameters pe mapped complete WHERE clause, expected maximum result rows, acceptable runtime target, relevant database indexes aur UP TO n ROWS use karte hue koi bhi row limits. Development ke baad, main actual ST05 trace results add karta hoon index usage aur database access time dikhate hue, jo performance regression testing ke liye baseline ban jaata hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Data selection documentation includes: primary and secondary tables with join types and conditions, complete WHERE clause mapped to selection screen parameters, expected maximum result rows, acceptable runtime target, relevant database indexes, and any row limits using UP TO n ROWS. After development, I add actual ST05 trace results showing index usage and database access time, which becomes the baseline for performance regression testing.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: TDS mein performance target nahi tha — developer ne bina UP TO ROWS clause ke SELECT likha. Production mein 500,000 rows load kar liya, timeout hua. TDS mein target likha hota: 'Max 100,000 rows, add UP TO 100000 ROWS' — issue prevent hota.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Not documenting performance targets in TDS — developers then write unlimited SELECTs that cause production timeouts.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TDS-documented SELECT — max 100k rows, index VBAK~2
" TDS note: Index VBAK~2 covers VKORG + ERDAT
SELECT vbeln, erdat, kunnr, netwr, waerk, vkorg
  FROM vbak
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_orders)
  WHERE erdat  IN @s_erdat     " TDS WHERE: selection screen range
    AND vkorg  IN @s_vkorg     " TDS WHERE: sales org filter
    AND auart  IN @s_auart     " TDS WHERE: order type
  UP TO 100000 ROWS.           " TDS performance target: max 100k
" ST05 trace result: 0.8s for 50k rows on index VBAK~2

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you document default ALV layout and sort criteria in TDS?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Default layout section yeh document karta hai: primary aur secondary sort columns ascending ya descending order ke saath; subtot flag ke saath sort criteria pe mapped subtotal break fields; custom toolbar function codes button label, icon aur action description ke saath; default column visibility jisme konse columns default hidden hain; aur default column widths. Yeh section TDS walkthrough ke dauran client ke saath review hota hai ensure karne ke liye ki report first run pe expected state mein open ho.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The default layout section documents: primary and secondary sort columns with ascending or descending order; subtotal break fields mapped to sort criteria with subtot flag; custom toolbar function codes with button label, icon, and action description; default column visibility with which columns are hidden by default; and default column widths. This section is reviewed with the client during TDS walkthrough to ensure the report opens in the expected state on first run.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Client bola 'report pehli baar open hone pe company code se sorted aur subtotals chahiye.' Bina TDS mein document kiye, developer ne kuch aur default banaya. UAT mein layout change request aaya. Layout section TDS mein hota toh client pehle hi confirm kar deta.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Not documenting default layout in TDS — client expects one default view, developer implements another. Change request costs 2-3 hours rework.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TDS-documented sort criteria and subtotals
DATA: ls_sort TYPE slis_sortinfo_alv,
      lt_sort TYPE slis_t_sortinfo_alv.
" TDS Sort Table: VKORG Ascending Priority 1 Subtotal YES
ls_sort-fieldname = 'VKORG'. ls_sort-spos = 1.
ls_sort-up = abap_true.      ls_sort-subtot = abap_true.
APPEND ls_sort TO lt_sort.   CLEAR ls_sort.
" TDS Sort Table: ERDAT Descending Priority 2 Subtotal NO
ls_sort-fieldname = 'ERDAT'. ls_sort-spos = 2.
ls_sort-down = abap_true.    ls_sort-subtot = ''.  
APPEND ls_sort TO lt_sort.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you document user command handling in ALV TDS?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: User command documentation ek table hai columns ke saath: function code, trigger type jaise toolbar button ya double-click, toolbar entries ke liye button label aur icon, aur action ka ek description. Actions typically include related transaction pe navigation, different data ke saath refreshing, detail aur summary view ke beech switching, ya modifications saving. Yeh table code mein USER_COMMAND event handler ke liye direct specification ban jaata hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: User command documentation is a table with columns: function code, trigger type such as toolbar button or double-click, button label and icon for toolbar entries, and a description of the action. Actions typically include navigation to a related transaction, refreshing with different data, switching between detail and summary view, or saving modifications. This table becomes the direct specification for the USER_COMMAND event handler in code.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Requirement: 'Double-click pe purchase order display kholna.' TDS mein function code ZPOD documented tha, action: CALL TRANSACTION ME23N with EBELN. Developer ne exactly implement kiya — no back-and-forth. Clean delivery.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Documenting only function codes without specifying what action they trigger — developer then interprets ambiguously.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TDS-documented USER_COMMAND handler
FORM user_command USING pv_ucomm TYPE sy-ucomm
                        ps_selfield TYPE slis_selfield.
  CASE pv_ucomm.
    WHEN 'ZDET'.   " TDS: Double-click → VA03 Sales Order Display
      SET PARAMETER ID 'AUN' FIELD ps_selfield-value.
      CALL TRANSACTION 'VA03' AND SKIP FIRST SCREEN.
    WHEN 'ZSUM'.   " TDS: Button 'Summary' → switch to summary view
      PERFORM build_summary_data.
      PERFORM display_summary_alv.
    WHEN OTHERS.   " Ignore unhandled commands
  ENDCASE.
ENDFORM.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you document background scheduling in Reports and ALV TDS?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Background scheduling documentation mein shamil hai: job mein use hone wala variant name, schedule frequency aur start time, output destination jaise SP01 viewing ke liye spool ya file output ke liye file path, spool retention period, alternative output handling ke liye program mein sy-batch detection hai ya nahi, aur job success aur failure ke liye notification approach. Yeh section typically BASIS team aur functional owners ke saath review hota hai jo scheduled jobs manage karenge.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Background scheduling documentation includes: variant name to be used in the job, schedule frequency and start time, output destination such as spool for SP01 viewing or file path for file output, spool retention period, whether the program has sy-batch detection for alternative output handling, and notification approach for job success and failure. This section is typically reviewed with the BASIS team and functional owners who will manage the scheduled jobs.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Project go-live ke baad background job schedule karna tha — TDS mein scheduling section nahi tha. BASIS team ko last minute koi nahi bata paya kaunsa variant use karna hai. Emergency call at 11 PM. Lesson: scheduling section TDS mein mandatory hai.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Not documenting the sy-batch handling in TDS — developer then ships a report that dumps in background. Always specify background mode behavior explicitly.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TDS Section 9: Background scheduling implementation
" TDS specifies: sy-batch → write CSV to /interfaces/sales/
CONSTANTS: c_output_path TYPE string
           VALUE '/interfaces/sales/monthly_report_'.

IF sy-batch = abap_true.
  " TDS: Background output to file
  DATA(lv_file) = c_output_path && sy-datum && '.csv'.
  PERFORM write_csv_output USING lv_file.
  MESSAGE |Report saved to { lv_file }| TYPE 'S'.
ELSE.
  " TDS: Foreground output via ALV grid
  PERFORM display_alv.
ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you get sign-off on a TDS for a Reports and ALV requirement?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: TDS sign-off mein shamil hai: pehle correctness ke liye team lead ke saath ek internal technical review complete karna; phir functional consultant ke saath ek walkthrough session schedule karna specifically selection screen fields aur output columns cover karte hue; document mein feedback incorporate karna; TDS attached ke saath written confirmation request karte hue ek formal email bhejna; aur confirmation email ko project documentation folder mein archive karna. Written sign-off development team ko scope creep aur UAT ke dauran undocumented requirement changes se protect karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: TDS sign-off involves: first completing an internal technical review with the team lead for correctness; then scheduling a walkthrough session with the functional consultant covering selection screen fields and output columns specifically; incorporating feedback into the document; sending a formal email with the TDS attached requesting written confirmation; and archiving the confirmation email in the project documentation folder. Written sign-off protects the development team from scope creep and undocumented requirement changes during UAT.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: UAT mein client bola 'ye column nahi chahiye tha, wo column chahiye tha.' Developer ne TDS sign-off email dikhaya — client ne 3 weeks pehle approve kiya tha. Result: change request as enhancement, not defect. Sign-off ne project ko bachaya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Getting verbal sign-off instead of written email confirmation — verbal agreements are not proof during UAT disputes.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" No code for sign-off process
" TDS template header to include in document:
" Program: ZSALES_MONTHLY_RPT
" Version: 1.0  Date: 2024-01-15
" Developer: [Name]  TL Review: [Name]
" Functional Sign-off: [Consultant Name]  Date: [Date]
" Basis Review: [Name]  Transport: DEVK9XXXXX
" Change History:
" v1.0 - Initial draft
" v1.1 - Added plant range per functional feedback
" v1.2 - Added authorization section per security review

followupAnswerBank.question: What should the authorization objects section in a Reports and ALV TDS contain?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Authorization objects section har required authorization object list karta hai, checked activity codes jaise display ke liye 03 ya create ke liye 01, check mein passed field values jaise company code ya plant, data selection ke relative program mein check kahan occur hota hai, aur authorization failure pe dikhaya message. Yeh section security team dwara review hota hai ensure karne ke liye ki saari required role assignments transport mein included hain aur koi data unauthorized users ko accessible nahi hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The authorization objects section lists each authorization object required, the activity codes checked such as 03 for display or 01 for create, the field values passed in the check such as company code or plant, where in the program the check occurs relative to data selection, and the message shown on authorization failure. This section is reviewed by the security team to ensure all required role assignments are included in the transport and that no data is accessible to unauthorized users.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Security audit mein pata chala ki report mein HR sensitive data tha but no authorization check for HR objects. TDS mein auth section blank tha — reviewer ne catch nahi kiya. Post-go-live security patch karna pada. Lesson: auth section peer-reviewed hona chahiye security team se.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Listing authorization objects without specifying which activity codes and field values are checked — implementation then misses critical authorization fields.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TDS Auth Section implementation
" Object: F_BKPF_BUK  Activity: 03  Field: BUKRS
AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
  ID 'BUKRS' FIELD p_bukrs
  ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  MESSAGE e001(z_msgs) WITH 'F_BKPF_BUK' p_bukrs.
ENDIF.
" Object: M_MATE_BUK  Activity: 03  Field: WERKS
IF s_werks IS NOT INITIAL.
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'M_MATE_WRX'
    ID 'WERKS' FIELD s_werks-low
    ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE e002(z_msgs) WITH 'M_MATE_WRX' s_werks-low.
  ENDIF.
ENDIF.

reports-alv-017.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: Effort estimation ka matlab hai — kaam shuru karne se pehle andaaza lagana ki kitna time lagega. Ye andaaza sahi hona bahut important hai — zyada estimate karo toh client unhappy, kam estimate karo toh tum unhappy (overtime!). Reports aur ALV ke liye industry-proven baselines hain. Sabse simple ALV list report — sirf data display, koi complex joins nahi, no special features — 2 to 3 days mein ban jaata hai. Jab joins zyaada ho jaayein, multiple tables se data aaye, functional specs complex ho — 3 to 5 days. Editable ALV jo data save bhi kare — 5 to 7 days kyunki validation aur BAPI calls extra kaam hai. Interactive drill-down (master-detail, clicking pe detail view) — 3 to 4 extra days. Background scheduling aur variant setup — 1 extra day. Testing ke liye development time ka 40% add karo — agar 5 days development hai, 2 days testing. Documentation ke liye 1 alag din. OO ALV mein custom toolbar buttons — 2 extra days. Ye sab numbers add karo aur wo tera estimate hai. Interview mein ye structure bologe toh interviewer clearly samjhega ki tum systematically sochte ho, randomly nahi.
reports-alv-017.hinglishMasterExplanation:
Effort estimation ek skill hai jo senior developers aur TLs ke liye bahut critical hai. Reports aur ALV estimation ke liye structured approach karo:

Step 1 — Complexity Classification: Pehle requirement ko classify karo. Simple display-only ALV (1-2 tables, standard fields) = Low complexity. ALV with complex joins, function module calls, calculated fields = Medium. Editable ALV with validation and save, or interactive drill-down = High. This classification sets your base estimate.

Step 2 — Base Development Estimates:
- Simple ALV list (1-2 tables, straightforward SELECT): 2-3 days
- Medium ALV (3-5 table joins, FAE, grouping, custom layout): 3-5 days
- Complex ALV (editable + save via BAPI, validation logic): 5-7 days
- Interactive/drill-down (double-click detail + navigation): base + 3-4 days
- Hierarchical ALV (tree view): base + 2-3 days

Step 3 — Add-on Estimates (add to base):
- Background scheduling + variant management: +1 day
- Custom toolbar buttons with OO ALV: +2 days
- Export to custom formatted Excel (ABAP2XLSX): +2-3 days
- Complex authorization checks (multiple objects): +0.5 day
- PDF output requirement: +1-2 days
- Performance optimization (large data volumes, ST05 tuning): +1-2 days

Step 4 — Testing Buffer: Development time ka 40% add karo. Ye include karta hai unit testing, integration testing, UAT support.

Step 5 — Documentation: 1 day fixed for TDS update, test script, transport documentation.

Step 6 — Risk Buffer: Unclear requirements ke liye 20% contingency. Dependencies pe (master data not ready, BASIS access not given) 10% add karo.

Step 7 — Communication: Estimate share karte waqt assumptions clearly bolna — 'This estimate assumes functional specification is complete, test data is available, and development system access is given on day 1.' Ye professional approach hai.
reports-alv-017.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'I use a structured effort estimation approach for Reports and ALV. The base estimate depends on complexity: a simple ALV display report takes 2-3 days, a medium complexity report with multiple joins and custom layout takes 3-5 days, and an editable ALV with save functionality takes 5-7 days. I then add incremental effort for interactive drill-down at 3-4 days, background scheduling at 1 day, and custom OO ALV toolbar at 2 days. Testing is always 40% of development effort, and documentation is 1 day fixed. I also include a 20% risk buffer for unclear requirements and state my assumptions clearly — complete functional specification, available test data, and day-one system access. This structured approach prevents underestimation and manages client expectations proactively.'

Hinglish tip: Interview mein specific numbers bolna bahut important hai — '2-3 days for simple ALV', '40% for testing' — ye numbers sunke interviewer samjhega ki tum field mein kaam kar chuke ho. Vague answers jaise 'depends on requirement' ke saath stop mat karo — always follow up with the structure.
reports-alv-017.codeExamples:
" Effort estimation complexity indicator — practical code patterns
" SIMPLE ALV (2-3 days): Basic SELECT + REUSE display
SELECT vbeln, erdat, kunnr, netwr
  FROM vbak INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_data)
  WHERE erdat IN @s_erdat.
CALL FUNCTION 'REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY'
  EXPORTING i_structure_name = 'VBAK'
  TABLES    t_outtab = lt_data.

" MEDIUM ALV (3-5 days): JOIN + field catalog + layout
SELECT a~vbeln, a~erdat, b~posnr, b~matnr, b~kwmeng
  FROM vbak AS a JOIN vbap AS b ON a~vbeln = b~vbeln
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_detail)
  WHERE a~erdat IN @s_erdat AND a~vkorg IN @s_vkorg.

" COMPLEX/EDITABLE ALV (5-7 days): DATA_CHANGED event
SET HANDLER lo_event_handler->on_data_changed
            FOR lo_grid
            ACTIVATION abap_true.
" Validation + BAPI call in event handler
" = significant additional complexity

reports-alv-017.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Structured effort estimation model for Reports and ALV deliveries.

Used for:
- Quoting development, testing and documentation time defensibly to PM and client
- Justifying add-ons like interactive drill-down, background scheduling and custom toolbar separately
- Communicating risk buffer, assumptions and dependencies up front
- Producing a written estimate breakdown that supports later Change Request discussions

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- Simple REUSE display report sits at 2 to 3 days base development
- Editable OO ALV with SET HANDLER on DATA_CHANGED plus BAPI save sits at 5 to 7 days
- Drill-down adds 3 to 4 days; OO ALV custom toolbar adds 2 days; background scheduling 1 day
- Testing is 40 percent of development; documentation is 1 day fixed; risk buffer 20 percent

Interview keyword: base plus add-ons plus 40 percent testing buffer

followupAnswerBank.question: How much effort does a simple ALV display report take?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek simple ALV display report — single ya two-table SELECT, standard field catalog, basic layout, no custom events — 2 se 3 development days leta hai. Day one TDS finalization, selection screen aur data fetch logic cover karta hai. Day two field catalog, ALV display, basic error handling aur initial testing cover karta hai. Day three refinements, performance check aur transport preparation handle karta hai. Testing effort development ka 40% hai, roughly 1 additional day add karta hai, total ko 3 se 4 days tak laata hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A simple ALV display report — single or two-table SELECT, standard field catalog, basic layout, no custom events — takes 2 to 3 development days. Day one covers TDS finalization, selection screen, and data fetch logic. Day two covers field catalog, ALV display, basic error handling, and initial testing. Day three handles refinements, performance check, and transport preparation. Testing effort is 40% of development, adding roughly 1 additional day, bringing the total to 3 to 4 days.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Sprint planning mein manager ne poochha: 'This straightforward sales order list report — how long?' Answer: '3 days development, 1 day testing, 1 day for UAT support = 5 days total. Assuming complete FS and test data available.' Manager appreciated the structured breakdown.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Quoting 1 day for a 'simple' report without considering field catalog, error handling, performance check, and transport preparation — always include the complete delivery effort.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Day 1 deliverable: Selection screen + data fetch
SELECT-OPTIONS: s_erdat FOR vbak-erdat DEFAULT sy-datum - 30
                                               TO sy-datum.
PARAMETERS: p_vkorg TYPE vkorg OBLIGATORY.

SELECT vbeln, erdat, kunnr, netwr, waerk
  FROM vbak INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_orders)
  WHERE erdat IN @s_erdat AND vkorg = @p_vkorg
  UP TO 50000 ROWS.
" Day 2: Build fcat, call ALV
" Day 3: Refinements + transport

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you estimate effort for an editable ALV with save functionality?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Save functionality ke saath editable ALV ke liye 5 se 7 development days required hain. Display reports se aage additional complexity aati hai: field catalog mein editable flags set karna, field-level validation ke saath DATA_CHANGED event handler implement karna, confirmation dialog ke saath ek save button create karna, ENQUEUE function modules ka use karke proper lock management ke saath BAPI ya UPDATE statement ke through actual save implement karna, aur validation failures aur partial saves ke liye comprehensive error handling. Testing development ka 40% hai, total ko 7 se 10 days tak laata hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Editable ALV with save functionality requires 5 to 7 development days. The additional complexity beyond display reports comes from: setting editable flags in field catalog, implementing the DATA_CHANGED event handler with field-level validation, creating a save button with confirmation dialog, implementing the actual save via BAPI or UPDATE statement with proper lock management using ENQUEUE function modules, and comprehensive error handling for validation failures and partial saves. Testing is 40% of development, bringing the total to 7 to 10 days.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Client estimate request: 'Editable ALV for updating delivery dates.' Initial quick estimate: 5 days. After analysis: DATA_CHANGED event (1 day), validation logic for 8 fields (1.5 days), BAPI_DELIVERYPROCESSING_EXEC call (1 day), lock management (0.5 day), error display in ALV (0.5 day) = 4.5 days + 40% testing = 6.5 days. Revised to 7 days with buffer.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Estimating editable ALV same as display ALV — the event handling, validation, and save logic typically doubles the effort.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Editable ALV complexity indicators
" These components add to estimation:
" 1. Edit flag in field catalog
ls_fcat-edit = abap_true.  " Adds field validation requirement

" 2. DATA_CHANGED event (1 day to implement)
METHODS: on_data_changed FOR EVENT data_changed
           OF cl_gui_alv_grid
           IMPORTING er_data_changed.

" 3. Lock before edit (0.5 day)
CALL FUNCTION 'ENQUEUE_EVVBAKE'
  EXPORTING vbeln = ls_sel-vbeln
  EXCEPTIONS foreign_lock = 1.

" 4. BAPI save (1 day including error handling)
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE'
  EXPORTING salesdocument = ls_row-vbeln.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you factor testing effort into Reports and ALV estimation?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Testing effort ek standard baseline ke roop mein development time ka 40% hai. 5-day development ke liye, 2 days testing ke liye add karo. Reports ke liye testing scope mein shamil hai: positive, negative aur edge cases jaise empty results aur maximum data volume cover karte hue unit testing; at least do different user profiles ke saath authorization testing; agar report background execution support karti hai toh background mode testing; production-representative data volumes ke saath performance testing; aur UAT support jo typically ek additional day require karta hai user acceptance testing ke dauran functional consultants ko support karne ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Testing effort is 40% of development time as a standard baseline. For a 5-day development, add 2 days for testing. Testing scope for reports includes: unit testing covering positive, negative, and edge cases such as empty results and maximum data volume; authorization testing with at least two different user profiles; background mode testing if the report supports background execution; performance testing with production-representative data volumes; and UAT support which typically requires one additional day for supporting functional consultants during user acceptance testing.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Project manager ne poocha: 'Why do you add 40% for testing?' Explanation: 'Last project mein testing include nahi ki estimate mein — delivery ke baad 3 days extra testing karna pada. Project mein delay. Ab 40% standard hai.' Manager accepted the rationale.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Quoting testing effort as 10% or 20% — insufficient for comprehensive testing including edge cases, authorization, and performance validation.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Test coverage checklist patterns
" Test 1: Empty result handling
IF lt_data IS INITIAL.
  MESSAGE 'No data found for selected criteria'
    TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'W'.
  LEAVE LIST-PROCESSING.  " Clean exit, no ALV dump
ENDIF.
" Test 2: Max data — add UP TO clause
UP TO 100000 ROWS.
" Test 3: Authorization failure
IF sy-subrc <> 0.  " After AUTHORITY-CHECK
  MESSAGE e001(z_auth).
ENDIF.
" Test 4: Background mode
IF sy-batch = abap_true. " Background output path
ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you estimate effort for adding interactive drill-down to an ALV report?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Interactive drill-down base estimate mein 3 se 4 days add karta hai. Additional effort mein shamil hai: main ALV mein DOUBLE_CLICK ya USER_COMMAND event handle karna; selected row ke key fields use karke detail level ke liye ek separate data selection query write karna; detail view ke liye ek separate field catalog aur ALV display build karna; main list pe wapis navigation manage karna; aur complete flow ka full integration testing. Three-level hierarchies ke liye, accordingly multiply karo — har level 3 se 4 days add karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Interactive drill-down adds 3 to 4 days to the base estimate. The additional effort includes: handling the DOUBLE_CLICK or USER_COMMAND event in the main ALV; writing a separate data selection query for the detail level using the selected row's key fields; building a separate field catalog and ALV display for the detail view; managing navigation back to the main list; and full integration testing of the complete flow. For three-level hierarchies, multiply accordingly — each level adds 3 to 4 days.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Requirement change during development: 'Add drill-down to line items from order header report.' Mid-sprint estimate: +3 days needed. Showed breakdown to manager — accepted. Lesson: always estimate drill-down explicitly even if requirement seems late addition.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Estimating drill-down as 1 day 'because it is just a double-click event' — the detail query, separate ALV, and navigation logic together take 3-4 days.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Interactive drill-down components — estimation basis
" Component 1: DOUBLE_CLICK event (0.5 day)
SET HANDLER lo_events->on_double_click
            FOR lo_grid ACTIVATION abap_true.

" Component 2: Detail SELECT (1 day)
METHOD on_double_click.
  DATA(lv_vbeln) = <header_row>-vbeln.
  SELECT posnr, matnr, kwmeng, netwr  " New query
    FROM vbap INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_items)
    WHERE vbeln = @lv_vbeln.

" Component 3: Detail ALV display (1 day)
  cl_salv_table=>factory(
    IMPORTING r_salv_table = DATA(lo_detail)
    CHANGING  t_table = lt_items ).
  lo_detail->display( ).
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you estimate effort for background scheduling and variant management?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Background scheduling estimate mein approximately 1 day add karta hai. Yeh cover karta hai: ABAP program mein sy-batch detection add karna background mode ke liye alternative output logic ke saath; standard selection parameters ke saath at least ek default variant create karna; production system mein job scheduling ke liye BASIS team ke saath coordinate karna; TDS mein variant name aur schedule document karna; aur foreground testing se separately background execution test karna. Different regions ya periods ke liye multiple scheduling variants require karne wali reports ke liye, ek additional half day add karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Background scheduling adds approximately 1 day to the estimate. This covers: adding sy-batch detection to the ABAP program with alternative output logic for background mode; creating at least one default variant with standard selection parameters; coordinating with the BASIS team for job scheduling in the production system; documenting the variant name and schedule in the TDS; and testing the background execution separately from foreground testing. For reports requiring multiple scheduling variants for different regions or periods, add an additional half day.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Go-live minus 2 days — BASIS team ne poocha 'kaunsa variant use karein job ke liye?' No variant prepared. Emergency variant creation + testing took half a day. Caused delay. Lesson: background scheduling is a distinct deliverable in the estimate.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Treating background scheduling as 'just 1-2 hours' — proper sy-batch handling, variant creation, BASIS coordination, and documentation together constitute a full day.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Background scheduling components — estimation basis
" Component 1: sy-batch detection
IF sy-batch = abap_true.
  " Component 2: Alternative output (spool)
  NEW-PAGE PRINT ON DESTINATION 'LP01'
                   IMMEDIATELY 'X'
                   KEEP IN SPOOL 'X'.
  LOOP AT lt_data INTO DATA(ls_row).
    WRITE: / ls_row-vbeln, ls_row-netwr.
  ENDLOOP.
  NEW-PAGE PRINT OFF.
ELSE.
  " Foreground: normal ALV
  PERFORM display_alv.
ENDIF.
" Component 3: Variant is created in SA38 (not coded)

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you communicate effort estimates to project managers and clients?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Effort estimates communicate karte waqt, main apni communication ko char parts mein structure karta hoon: pehle, main assumptions clearly state karta hoon jaise complete functional specification, available test data aur no access delays; doosra, main ek broken-down estimate provide karta hoon development, testing, documentation aur UAT support separate karte hue; teesra, main ek range deta hoon single number nahi — example 5 se 7 days — requirement ambiguity account karne ke liye; aur chautha, main verbal discussion ke immediately baad email ya meeting minutes mein estimate document karta hoon. Yeh approach misunderstandings prevent karta hai aur ek traceable record create karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When communicating effort estimates, I structure my communication in four parts: first, I state assumptions clearly such as complete functional specification, available test data, and no access delays; second, I provide a broken-down estimate separating development, testing, documentation, and UAT support; third, I give a range not a single number — for example 5 to 7 days — to account for requirement ambiguity; and fourth, I document the estimate in an email or meeting minutes immediately after verbal discussion. This approach prevents misunderstandings and creates a traceable record.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Client ne estimate mein add-ons maange mid-project — 'interactive drill-down add karo, estimate same rehni chahiye.' Documented estimate email mein tha — showed original scope vs new request. Change request process follow hua — extra 3 days approved. Without written estimate, scope creep hota.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Giving a single-point estimate like '5 days exactly' instead of a range — then being held accountable when it takes 6 days due to legitimate complexity.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Estimation breakdown template (not code — document format)
" === EFFORT ESTIMATE: ZSALES_MONTHLY_RPT ===
" Assumptions: FS v1.2 signed, test data in DEV, access from Day1
"
" Development Breakdown:
" - TDS finalization + selection screen:  0.5 day
" - Data selection + JOIN logic:           1.0 day
" - Field catalog + ALV display:           1.0 day
" - Custom toolbar + sort/subtotals:       0.5 day
" - Error handling + authorization check:  0.5 day
" Subtotal Development:                    3.5 days
"
" Testing (40% of dev):                    1.5 days
" Documentation:                           1.0 day
" Total Estimate:                       5-6 days
" Risk Buffer (20% for unclear reqs):  +1 day
" === FINAL: 6-7 DAYS ===

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you revise an estimate when requirements change mid-development?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab requirements mid-development mein change hote hain, main ek formal change request process follow karta hoon. Pehle, main TDS se original agreed scope aur new request clearly document karta hoon. Doosra, main change ke liye delta effort estimate karta hoon — development, testing aur existing code mein koi bhi rework. Teesra, main ek Change Request document raise karta hoon original scope, new scope aur additional effort in days state karte hue. Chautha, main change start karne se pehle written approval leta hoon. Yeh process transparency maintain karke aur silent scope creep prevent karke developer aur client dono ko protect karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When requirements change mid-development, I follow a formal change request process. First, I document the original agreed scope from the TDS and the new request clearly. Second, I estimate the delta effort for the change — development, testing, and any rework to existing code. Third, I raise a Change Request document stating original scope, new scope, and additional effort in days. Fourth, I get written approval before starting the change. This process protects both the developer and the client by maintaining transparency and preventing silent scope creep.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Day 4 of 5-day estimate — functional bola 'add one more table JOIN and three calculated fields.' Impact: +2 days easily. Raised CR immediately — showed existing code, impact analysis, revised estimate. Client approved. Delivered in 7 days with CR approval. No complaint.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Silently absorbing requirement changes without raising a CR — client then expects same timeline, developer does overtime, quality suffers.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Change Request impact — new JOIN added mid-development
" Original: SELECT from VBAK only (estimated)
" Change Request: Add KNA1 JOIN for customer name + city
" CR Delta: +0.5 day development, +0.25 day testing

" Original SELECT (done)
SELECT vbeln, erdat, kunnr, netwr
  FROM vbak INTO TABLE @lt_orders
  WHERE erdat IN @s_erdat.

" Post-CR change — new JOIN
SELECT a~vbeln, a~erdat, a~kunnr, a~netwr,
       b~name1 AS customer_name, b~ort01 AS city
  FROM vbak AS a
  LEFT JOIN kna1 AS b ON a~kunnr = b~kunnr
  INTO TABLE @lt_orders
  WHERE a~erdat IN @s_erdat.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you estimate effort for OO ALV with custom toolbar buttons?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Simple display reports ke liye CL_SALV_TABLE use karne wala OO ALV REUSE compared mein minimal overhead add karta hai — ek straightforward report ke liye approximately 1 se 2 development days. However, CL_GUI_ALV_GRID use karne wale custom toolbar buttons approximately 2 days add karte hain: custom container aur grid object setup create karna, toolbar mein function keys register karna, icons ke saath custom buttons add karne ke liye TOOLBAR event implement karna, aur button actions ke liye USER_COMMAND event implement karna. Har additional custom button implementation aur testing ke liye about half a day add karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: OO ALV using CL_SALV_TABLE for simple display reports adds minimal overhead compared to REUSE — approximately 1 to 2 development days for a straightforward report. However, custom toolbar buttons using CL_GUI_ALV_GRID add approximately 2 days: creating custom container and grid object setup, registering function keys in the toolbar, implementing the TOOLBAR event to add custom buttons with icons, and implementing the USER_COMMAND event for button actions. Each additional custom button adds about half a day for implementation and testing.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Requirement: OO ALV with 3 custom buttons — Details, Summary, Export. Estimate: base OO ALV 3 days + custom toolbar 2 days + 3 buttons testing 1 day = 6 days development + 2.5 days testing = 8.5 days total. Communicated to PM with breakdown — approved.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Estimating OO ALV same as REUSE ALV without accounting for custom container, toolbar registration, and event handler complexity.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" OO ALV custom toolbar — estimation components
" Component 1: Container + grid setup (0.5 day)
CREATE OBJECT lo_cont EXPORTING container_name = 'CC_MAIN'.
CREATE OBJECT lo_grid EXPORTING i_parent = lo_cont.

" Component 2: Toolbar button registration (0.5 day)
SET HANDLER lo_eh->on_toolbar FOR lo_grid ACTIVATION abap_true.
" In handler:
DATA(ls_btn) = VALUE stb_button(
  function = 'ZSUM' text = 'Summary'
  icon     = '@0A@'  butn_type = 0 ).
APPEND ls_btn TO e_object->mt_toolbar.

" Component 3: USER_COMMAND handler (0.5 day)
SET HANDLER lo_eh->on_user_command FOR lo_grid ACTIVATION abap_true.

reports-alv-018.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: SAP ABAP developer ke liye kuch transactions hain jo daily use hote hain — inhe jaana mandatory hai, especially Reports aur ALV development ke context mein. Group 1 — Development transactions: SE38 (ABAP Editor — report likhne ka main jagah), SE80 (Object Navigator — modern IDE-style development), SE11 (Data Dictionary — table structures check karna, field names verify karna). Group 2 — Performance transactions: ST05 (SQL Trace — kaunsa SQL slow hai check karo), SAT (Runtime Analysis — program ka execution time detail mein dekho), SM50/SM66 (Work Process monitor). Group 3 — Testing aur execution: SA38 (report execute karo bina transaction code ke), SE93 (transaction code create karo report ke liye), SE16N (table data check karo for testing). Group 4 — Background aur output: SM36 (job schedule karo), SM37 (job status dekho), SP01 (spool output dekho). Group 5 — Authorization aur errors: SU53 (authorization check failures dekho), SE91 (message class create karo custom messages ke liye). Group 6 — Code quality: ATC (ABAP Test Cockpit — code quality check). Ye sab transactions interview mein poochhe jaate hain — har ek ka use case clarity se bolna chahiye.
reports-alv-018.hinglishMasterExplanation:
Reports aur ALV se connected SAP transactions ko categories mein organize karo — interview mein structured answer dene ke liye:

Category 1 — Development Tools:
- SE38: ABAP Editor. Ye primary transaction hai report development ke liye. Yahan program create, edit, activate, aur execute karte hain. Syntax check, code hints, breakpoints sab yahan.
- SE80: Object Navigator — ABAP Workbench. Function groups, classes, includes, message classes — sab ek jagah manage karo. Modern way of development.
- SE11: ABAP Dictionary. Table structures, data elements, domains, search helps — ye sab define aur check karte hain. Report likhte waqt field names aur types verify karne ke liye daily use.

Category 2 — Performance Analysis:
- ST05: SQL Trace. Report run karo ST05 active rakhke, phir trace dekhke pata chalega kaunsa SELECT kitna time le raha hai, kaunsa index use ho raha hai ya table scan ho raha hai. Performance issues diagnose karne ke liye critical.
- SAT: ABAP Runtime Analysis (Transaction SAT, modern replacement of SE30). Full program execution profiling — kaunsi method/routine kitna time le rahi hai. ABAP performance bottlenecks dhundhne ke liye.

Category 3 — Execution aur Transport:
- SA38: Report execute karo without creating a transaction code. Testing ke liye perfect. QA aur production mein bhi use hota hai.
- SE93: Transaction code maintain karo. Report complete hone ke baad user-friendly tcode create karo — jaise ZMM01 for materials report.
- STMS: Transport Management System — transports release aur import karo.

Category 4 — Background Jobs:
- SM36: Background job schedule karo. Variant select karo, time set karo, server set karo. Functional aur BASIS team ye use karte hain.
- SM37: Background job status monitor karo. Job log dekho, spool output access karo. Scheduled, Active, Finished, Cancelled status.
- SP01: Spool request management. Background job ka output yahan milta hai. Print preview, download, reprint.

Category 5 — Data Verification aur Testing:
- SE16N: Table contents check karo. Report ke expected output verify karne ke liye test data confirm karo. WHERE conditions directly test karo.
- SQVI: Quick viewer — functional team ke liye simple queries without ABAP.

Category 6 — Authorization:
- SU53: Authorization check display. Agar user ko data nahi dikh raha — SU53 pe jaao, last failed authorization check dikhega. Developer ke liye diagnosis tool.

Category 7 — Code Quality:
- ATC (ABAP Test Cockpit): Code quality checks run karo — security vulnerabilities, performance issues, coding conventions. SE38 se directly invoke kar sakte ho. Mandatory in many projects before transport.
reports-alv-018.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'For Reports and ALV development, I regularly use three groups of transactions. For development: SE38 or SE80 for coding, SE11 for table and field research. For performance analysis: ST05 for SQL trace to identify slow queries and missing indexes, and SAT for ABAP runtime profiling. For testing and operations: SA38 to execute reports, SE16N to verify test data, SM37 to check background job status, SP01 to view spool output, SU53 to diagnose authorization failures, and ATC for code quality checks before transport. I also use SE93 to create transaction codes and SE91 for custom message classes. These transactions cover the complete lifecycle from development to production support.'

Hinglish tip: Interview mein transactions bolte waqt sirf naam mat bolna — brief context bhi do. 'ST05 for SQL trace to find slow queries' sunne pe interviewer samjhega ki tum practically use karte ho, sirf list yaad nahi ki.
reports-alv-018.codeExamples:
" SE11: Field research — verify table/field before coding
" Always check SE11 before writing field names in SELECT
" VBAK-ERDAT = Order Creation Date (DATS, length 8)
" VBAK-NETWR = Net Value (CURR, ref currency VBAK-WAERK)

" ST05 friendly SELECT — uses index, avoids full table scan
" ST05 trace will show: index VBAK~2 used, time < 1s
SELECT vbeln, erdat, kunnr, netwr, waerk
  FROM vbak
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_orders)
  WHERE vkorg = @p_vkorg        " Index field first
    AND erdat IN @s_erdat        " Narrows result set
  UP TO 100000 ROWS.

" SE16N equivalent in ABAP — data verification
" During testing: verify expected rows in SE16N with same WHERE
" SELECT COUNT(*) FROM vbak WHERE vkorg = 'IN01'
"   AND erdat BETWEEN '20240101' AND '20241231'
" SE16N result should match your lt_orders count

" SU53 scenario: AUTHORITY-CHECK for company code
AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
  ID 'BUKRS' FIELD p_bukrs ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  " User will see failed auth in SU53 after this
  MESSAGE e001(zmsg) WITH p_bukrs.
ENDIF.
reports-alv-018.interviewScriptEnglish: For Reports and ALV development, I regularly use three groups of transactions. For development: SE38 or SE80 for coding, SE11 for table and field research. For performance analysis: ST05 for SQL trace to identify slow queries and missing indexes, and SAT for ABAP runtime profiling. For testing and operations: SA38 to execute reports, SE16N to verify test data, SM37 to check background job status, SP01 to view spool output, SU53 to diagnose authorization failures, and ATC for code quality checks before transport. I also use SE93 to create transaction codes and SE91 for custom message classes. These transactions cover the complete lifecycle from development to production support.
